mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above its 12-month trend, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days without requiring threshold activation.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to produce positive returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS > 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% indicates tight labor market conditions that accelerate wage growth and consumer purchasing power, driving retail sales (RSAFS) higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when this threshold is breached.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE=3.4% and RSAFS=696982.0 million dollars
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: The joint signal (UNRATE < 3.5% AND RSAFS growth) fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 1.5% over a 3-month period signals weakening economic activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the directional accuracy of the signal falls below 50% over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 1,400 thousand units annually signals strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence, which cascades into construction employment and materials demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in expansion regimes.
regime_hint: economic expansion with accommodative monetary conditions
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following HOUST > 1400 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC falls below 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation rate) turning negative signals monetary accommodation that reduces the opportunity cost of equity holding relative to cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when this regime shift occurs.
regime_hint: monetary policy transition from tightening to accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal's Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the directional accuracy of the signal falls below 52% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue expansion and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days when both indicators show concurrent strength.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1=20198.091 billion chained 2017 dollars and RSAFS=696982.0 million dollars
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.07 over any 2-year rolling window.
